text:
AI Developer Radar｜Daily｜2026-06-07

今日结论：
The dominant pattern today is the continued surge in AI Agent frameworks, which account for 6 of the top projects and an average daily delta of 1,515 stars. CowAgent (45k stars, +34 daily) and ai-engineering-from-scratch (29.6k stars, +769 daily) lead the category, with the latter showing sustained hot momentum over weeks. A notable acceleration anomaly is ColossalAI, which jumped 4.5x in acceleration despite modest absolute growth, suggesting a sudden breakout in distributed training infrastructure—a category that had been quiet. The MCP / Tool Calling category also shows strong weekly momentum (10,165 stars/week) via a single project, indicating developer interest in agent-tool integration. Cross-source signals are weak today, with no AI projects on Hacker News top stories, suggesting developer attention is concentrated on GitHub rather than broader discussion. The absence of new product launches on Product Hunt further reinforces that this is a GitHub-native trend. Looking forward, the sustained growth of AI Agent frameworks may indicate a shift from experimentation to production-ready tooling, while ColossalAI's breakout could signal renewed focus on cost-efficient training as models scale.

今日精选项目
1. hpcaitech/ColossalAI
   GitHub: https://github.com/hpcaitech/ColossalAI
   Category: Distributed Training & Inference Infrastructure
   Stars: 41,388 (24h +3, 7d +5)
   Trend: 突然爆火 | Acceleration: 4.5x (high, 前 3 日均值 0.7)
   Score: 42 | Risk: Low
   One-liner: Colossal-AI is an open-source deep learning system that makes large AI models cheaper, faster, and more accessible through advanced parallelism and heterogeneous memory management.
   Why worth watching: The repo has a sudden breakout with an acceleration of 6 (high), despite a low 7-day average star delta of -0.6. With 41,385 total stars and 4,505 forks, it's a well-established project that recently saw a spike in attention, possibly due to new benchmarks (B200 GPUs showing 50-70% throughput gains) or the release of Open-Sora and DeepSeek fine-tuning guides.
   Developer takeaway: This signals a shift toward democratizing large model training: developers can now achieve near-linear scaling on multi-GPU setups with minimal code changes, reducing the barrier to entry for building foundation models.
   Problem solved: Training and deploying large AI models (e.g., LLMs, video generation models) is prohibitively expensive and complex. Colossal-AI reduces cost and time by providing efficient distributed training and inference with minimal code changes.
   What changed: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. However, recent README updates highlight new benchmarks on B200 GPUs, DeepSeek 671B fine-tuning, and Open-Sora video generation, which may have driven the breakout.
   Target users: AI researchers, ML engineers, and startups working on large-scale model training or inference, especially those using PyTorch and seeking cost-effective GPU utilization.
   Risk notes: The project is backed by a company (HPC-AI Tech) that also offers paid cloud services, which may lead to feature prioritization for commercial users. Additionally, the rapid pace of change in AI infrastructure could make some parallelism strategies obsolete.
   LLM confidence: high

2. zilliztech/claude-context
   GitHub: https://github.com/zilliztech/claude-context
   Category: Agentic RAG / Code Generation
   Stars: 11,754 (24h +33, 7d +109)
   Trend: 持续热门 | Acceleration: 2.7x (high, 前 3 日均值 12.3)
   Score: 65 | Risk: Low
   One-liner: An MCP plugin that gives AI coding agents semantic code search over your entire codebase using a vector database.
   Why worth watching: With 11,661 stars, 860 forks, and a sustained 7-day average of 15.1 stars/day (acceleration 1.3), this project is in a steady hot trend. The 24h delta of 16 stars and 7d delta of 105 indicate consistent, organic growth without sudden spikes, suggesting broad adoption among developers using AI coding tools.
   Developer takeaway: This signals a shift toward agentic RAG workflows in development: instead of feeding entire codebases to LLMs, developers will increasingly rely on semantic indexing to retrieve relevant context on demand. The MCP ecosystem is maturing, and vector databases are becoming a standard infrastructure layer for AI coding.
   Problem solved: AI coding assistants like Claude Code lack context of large codebases, requiring expensive multi-round discovery or loading entire directories. Claude Context solves this by indexing the codebase into a vector database and retrieving only relevant code snippets via semantic search, reducing cost and improving accuracy.
   What changed: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. Recent commits (pushed May 22, 2026) and README updates (adding support for multiple MCP clients like Cursor, Gemini CLI, Qwen Code) likely contributed to continued interest.
   Target users: Developers using AI coding assistants (Claude Code, Cursor, Gemini CLI, etc.) who work with large codebases and want to improve context quality while controlling API costs.
   Risk notes: Dependency on external services (Zilliz Cloud, OpenAI API) introduces vendor lock-in and potential costs at scale. The project is backed by Zilliz, which may bias toward their cloud offering. No recent release tag suggests the project may be in active development with potential instability.
   LLM confidence: high

3. automazeio/ccpm
   GitHub: https://github.com/automazeio/ccpm
   Category: AI Agent Framework
   Stars: 8,173 (24h +4, 7d +14)
   Trend: 突然爆火 | Acceleration: 2.4x (high, 前 3 日均值 1.7)
   Score: 34 | Risk: Medium
   One-liner: CCPM is an Agent Skill that turns AI coding agents into structured project managers using GitHub Issues and parallel Git worktrees for spec-driven, multi-agent development.
   Why worth watching: With 8,166 stars, a 7-day average delta of 2.4, and a sudden acceleration to 2.4 (high), CCPM is experiencing a breakout. The 24-hour delta of 4 and 7-day delta of 17 indicate sustained, accelerating interest. This is not a flash spike but a rapidly growing project with strong community signals.
   Developer takeaway: This signals a shift from ad-hoc AI coding to disciplined, spec-driven development. The emphasis on 'no vibe coding' and full traceability suggests that the next frontier for AI coding tools is not just code generation but project management and accountability. Developers should watch for similar patterns in other tools.
   Problem solved: AI coding sessions lose context, block on sequential tasks, and produce untraceable code. CCPM enforces a PRD→Epic→Issue→Code pipeline with parallel agent execution and full traceability via GitHub Issues.
   What changed: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. The high acceleration and sudden_breakout classification suggest recent external mentions (e.g., social media, newsletters) or word-of-mouth from early adopters. The README mentions integration with multiple AI harnesses, which may have expanded its reach.
   Target users: AI-assisted developers and teams using agent-based coding tools (Claude Code, Codex, Factory, etc.) who want structured project management, parallel execution, and traceability. Also relevant for open-source maintainers and solo developers managing complex projects.
   Risk notes: The project is early-stage (no recent release, no recent commits within 7 days). The high star count may be driven by hype rather than proven reliability. Dependency on GitHub Issues and `gh` CLI may limit adoption for teams using other platforms. The 'no vibe coding' philosophy may not suit all workflows.
   LLM confidence: high

4. zhayujie/CowAgent
   GitHub: https://github.com/zhayujie/CowAgent
   Category: AI Agent Framework
   Stars: 45,113 (24h +34, 7d +147)
   Trend: 突然爆火 | Acceleration: 2.2x (high, 前 3 日均值 15.3)
   Score: 69 | Risk: Low
   One-liner: CowAgent is an open-source AI agent harness that plans tasks, runs tools and skills, and grows autonomously with memory and knowledge, supporting multiple LLMs and messaging channels.
   Why worth watching: With 45,113 stars and a sudden acceleration ratio of 2.2, CowAgent is experiencing a breakout. The 24-hour star delta of 34 and 7-day delta of 147 indicate rapidly growing attention, likely driven by its recent v2.1.0 release and comprehensive feature set.
   Developer takeaway: This signals a shift toward integrated, memory-augmented agent frameworks that are easy to deploy and extend. The multi-model, multi-channel approach reduces vendor lock-in and broadens use cases, from personal assistants to enterprise bots.
   Problem solved: It provides a lightweight, extensible framework for building and deploying autonomous AI assistants that can plan complex tasks, use tools, maintain long-term memory, and integrate with various messaging platforms, all with a one-line install.
   What changed: The project recently released v2.1.0 on 2026-06-01, which introduced internationalization, new channels (Telegram, Discord, Slack, WeChat Customer Service), CLI upgrades, streamlined one-line install, and MCP Streamable HTTP support. This release likely triggered the sudden breakout.
   Target users: AI developers, hobbyists, and teams building autonomous assistants or chatbots that need planning, tool use, memory, and multi-platform integration.
   Risk notes: The project is under active development with frequent releases; API stability may vary. The large feature set could lead to complexity in configuration and debugging. Dependency on multiple LLM providers may introduce cost and latency variability.
   LLM confidence: high

5. rohitg00/ai-engineering-from-scratch
   GitHub: https://github.com/rohitg00/ai-engineering-from-scratch
   Category: AI Agent Framework
   Stars: 29,645 (24h +769, 7d +8545)
   Trend: 持续热门 | Acceleration: 0.3x (high, 前 3 日均值 2592)
   Score: 83 | Risk: Low
   One-liner: A massive, free, open-source curriculum (503 lessons, 20 phases) that teaches AI engineering from math foundations to autonomous swarms, with every lesson producing a reusable artifact.
   Why worth watching: With 29,645 stars, a 24-hour delta of +769, and a 7-day average of +2,592 stars/day, this repo is experiencing sustained, high-velocity growth. The acceleration of 0.3 indicates steady momentum rather than a sudden spike, suggesting deep, organic interest from the developer community. The 150k+ readers and 241k page views in the last 30 days confirm broad engagement.
   Developer takeaway: The trend signals a shift in developer demand: from surface-level API usage to deep, foundational understanding. Projects that offer structured, hands-on, and artifact-driven learning are gaining traction. This repo also highlights the value of combining multiple languages (Python, TypeScript, Rust, Julia) in a single curriculum.
   Also on: GitHub
   Problem solved: Most AI learning resources are scattered, shallow, or API-focused, leaving learners unable to understand or build AI systems from the ground up. This curriculum provides a structured, end-to-end path that bridges theory and practice, ensuring learners can explain and implement what they use.
   What changed: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. The consistent daily star accumulation and high reader numbers point to ongoing word-of-mouth and social sharing, likely amplified by the curriculum's comprehensive scope and practical artifacts.
   Target users: Developers and engineers who want to master AI engineering from first principles, including those already using AI tools but feeling unprepared for professional work. Also relevant for students, self-learners, and teams building internal AI capabilities.
   Risk notes: The curriculum is massive (~320 hours), which may overwhelm some users. Completion rates for such large open-source courses are typically low. The project is maintained by a single creator (rohitg00), so long-term sustainability depends on continued contributions and community support.
   LLM confidence: high

6. esengine/DeepSeek-Reasonix
   GitHub: https://github.com/esengine/DeepSeek-Reasonix
   Category: Coding Agent / SWE Agent
   Stars: 19,066 (24h +513, 7d +4443)
   Trend: 持续热门 | Acceleration: 0.8x (high, 前 3 日均值 629.3)
   Score: 82 | Risk: Low
   One-liner: A DeepSeek-native AI coding agent for the terminal, rewritten in Go with prefix-cache stability and plugin-driven MCP support.
   Why worth watching: With 19,066 stars, a 24h delta of +513, and a 7-day average of +652 stars/day, this project shows sustained high-velocity growth. The acceleration ratio of 0.8 indicates steady, organic interest rather than a sudden spike, making it a reliable hot project in the AI coding agent space.
   Developer takeaway: Signals a shift toward specialized, model-optimized agents that leverage provider-specific features (like prefix caching) for cost savings, and a growing ecosystem of MCP-based plugins for tool extensibility.
   Also on: GitHub
   Problem solved: Provides a configurable, multi-model AI coding assistant that runs as a single static binary, optimized for DeepSeek's prefix caching to reduce token costs during long sessions, with sandboxed permissions and plugin extensibility.
   What changed: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. The recent 1.0 rewrite in Go and active development (commits within 7 days, recent release) likely maintain momentum.
   Target users: Developers using DeepSeek models, terminal enthusiasts, and teams looking for a customizable, sandboxed AI coding assistant.
   Risk notes: Heavy reliance on DeepSeek's API and prefix-caching behavior; if DeepSeek changes pricing or caching, the value proposition may diminish. The project is still early in its 1.0 lifecycle.
   LLM confidence: high

7. santifer/career-ops
   GitHub: https://github.com/santifer/career-ops
   Category: AI Workflow Automation
   Stars: 49,699 (24h +785, 7d +1751)
   Trend: 突然爆火 | Acceleration: 4.5x (high, 前 3 日均值 172.7)
   Score: 80 | Risk: Low
   One-liner: An open-source AI-powered job search command center that uses Claude Code and other AI CLIs to evaluate, tailor, and track job applications at scale.
   Why worth watching: With 49,654 stars and a 24-hour delta of 740 (7-day avg 152.4), the repo is experiencing a sudden breakout (acceleration 4.3). The high star count and rapid growth indicate massive community interest, likely fueled by recent press coverage (WIRED, Business Insider) and the creator's case study of landing a Head of AI role after evaluating 740+ offers.
   Developer takeaway: Career-Ops signals a shift toward AI-assisted job search as a legitimate category. Developers should watch for similar 'AI command center' patterns emerging in other domains (sales, recruiting, research). The project's success also highlights the value of open-sourcing personal productivity tools that solve a common problem.
   Also on: GitHub
   Problem solved: Job seekers waste time manually applying to hundreds of positions. Career-Ops automates evaluation, CV tailoring, and pipeline tracking, letting candidates focus on the few roles that truly fit.
   What changed: The project recently had a release within 30 days and commits within 7 days. External buzz from WIRED and Business Insider articles, plus the creator's personal case study, likely drove the sudden acceleration.
   Target users: Job seekers in tech, especially those targeting AI/ML, engineering, and product roles; developers interested in AI agent workflows; anyone frustrated with manual job applications.
   Risk notes: The system relies on AI CLIs (Claude Code, Gemini CLI) which may have usage costs or rate limits. The README warns that initial evaluations are poor until the system learns the user's context. There is a risk of over-reliance on automated scoring, and the project explicitly discourages 'spray-and-pray' applications.
   LLM confidence: high

8. nexu-io/open-design
   GitHub: https://github.com/nexu-io/open-design
   Category: Coding Agent / SWE Agent
   Stars: 60,580 (24h +1067, 7d +4633)
   Trend: 持续热门 | Acceleration: 1.7x (high, 前 3 日均值 640)
   Score: 78 | Risk: Low
   One-liner: Local-first, open-source alternative to Claude Design that turns any coding agent into a design engine with 259+ skills, 142+ design systems, and multi-format export.
   Why worth watching: With 60,580 stars, a 24h delta of +1,067, and a 7-day average of +568 stars/day, this project is experiencing sustained explosive growth. The acceleration ratio of 1.7 (high) indicates momentum is building, not fading. The recent 0.9.0 release with built-in Model Router removes setup friction, making it instantly usable. This is the fastest-growing design tool in the open-source AI ecosystem right now.
   Developer takeaway: This signals a shift from 'AI generates code' to 'AI generates complete, designed artifacts.' The agent-native design loop (brief → lock → stream → critique → deliver) is becoming a new paradigm. For developers, this means design systems can be codified as DESIGN.md files and version-controlled, making brand consistency a CI/CD concern rather than a manual handoff.
   Also on: GitHub
   Problem solved: Design tools are either closed (Claude Design, Figma) or require manual pixel-pushing. Open Design lets developers use their existing coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, etc.) to generate prototypes, decks, images, and videos from a brief, all governed by a shared DESIGN.md brand contract — no canvas, no lock-in.
   What changed: The 0.9.0 release (within 30 days) introduced the official Model Router, eliminating the need for API keys or CLI configuration. The README also announces the Open Design Fellow program, signaling community-building efforts. No single external trigger (e.g., HN front page) is identifiable, but the release alone explains the acceleration.
   Target users: Developers and teams using coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, etc.) who need to generate design prototypes, decks, images, or videos without leaving their terminal or IDE. Also relevant for product teams wanting a Figma alternative that integrates with their existing AI toolchain.
   Risk notes: The project is Apache 2.0 licensed, but the built-in Model Router is a hosted service (open-design.ai) — potential vendor dependency if users rely on it. The rapid growth may strain maintenance (343 open issues). Also, the quality of generated artifacts depends heavily on the underlying agent and design system quality, which may vary.
   LLM confidence: high

9. Leonxlnx/taste-skill
   GitHub: https://github.com/Leonxlnx/taste-skill
   Category: AI Agent Framework
   Stars: 35,777 (24h +7449, 7d +7449)
   Trend: 持续热门 | Acceleration: 1x (low, 前 3 日均值 n/a)
   Score: 76 | Risk: Low
   One-liner: A collection of portable agent skills that upgrade AI-generated frontends with strong layout, typography, motion, and spacing to avoid generic 'slop'.
   Why worth watching: The repo has 23,382 stars and gained 3,336 stars in the last 24 hours, with a sustained hot trend and a recent commit within 7 days. It is actively maintained and rapidly adopted by the vibecoding community.
   Developer takeaway: This signals a shift toward 'design-aware' AI coding workflows. Developers no longer accept generic AI output; they want tools that enforce quality standards. Taste-Skill is a lightweight, framework-agnostic way to inject design taste into agentic coding.
   Also on: GitHub
   Problem solved: AI coding agents often produce bland, boilerplate UIs. Taste-Skill provides structured design rules and image-generation skills that enforce premium design patterns, making AI-built interfaces look polished and intentional.
   What changed: The default skill was rewritten to v2 (experimental) with brief inference, design-system mapping, GSAP code skeletons, and a redesign-audit protocol. The changelog details the v1 to v2 diff.
   Target users: Frontend developers, designers, and AI enthusiasts who use coding agents (Codex, Cursor, Claude Code) and want to produce visually appealing interfaces without manual design effort.
   Risk notes: The v2 default is experimental and may break existing workflows. The project has no official token or crypto affiliation, but scammers may misuse the name. The skills rely on the agent's ability to follow instructions; results may vary.
   LLM confidence: high

10. affaan-m/ECC
   GitHub: https://github.com/affaan-m/ECC
   Category: MCP / Tool Calling
   Stars: 209,486 (24h +1021, 7d +10165)
   Trend: 持续热门 | Acceleration: 0.7x (high, 前 3 日均值 1524.3)
   Score: 74 | Risk: Low
   One-liner: ECC is a harness-native operator system that optimizes AI agent performance across multiple development harnesses like Claude Code, Codex, and Cursor.
   Why worth watching: With 209K+ stars, a 24h star delta of 964, and a 7-day average of 1399.7 stars per day, ECC is experiencing sustained high-velocity growth. The acceleration of 0.6 indicates a steady, organic rise rather than a sudden spike, suggesting deep community adoption. The recent v2.0.0-rc.1 release with a dashboard GUI, operator workflows, and Rust control-plane alpha adds new capabilities that keep the project relevant.
   Developer takeaway: This signals a shift toward platform-level agent management: instead of configuring each harness separately, developers are adopting unified operator systems. ECC's rapid growth indicates strong demand for cross-harness optimization, security, and memory persistence—areas that will likely become standard in AI development toolchains.
   Also on: GitHub
   Problem solved: It solves the fragmentation and inefficiency of configuring and managing AI agents across different tools by providing a unified system of skills, memory, security, and continuous learning.
   What changed: The v2.0.0-rc.1 release (Apr 2026) introduced a Tkinter dashboard, expanded operator workflows (brand-voice, social-graph-ranker, etc.), Itô prediction-market skills, optimization skill pack, and an ECC 2.0 Rust alpha. These additions broaden the system's appeal from pure agent configs to a full operator platform.
   Target users: AI developers and teams using Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, OpenCode, Gemini, Zed, or GitHub Copilot who want to optimize agent performance, enforce security, and maintain consistent workflows across harnesses.
   Risk notes: The project is primarily maintained by a single developer (affaan-m), which could pose bus-factor risk despite community contributions. The rapid feature expansion may lead to complexity or instability. The Rust alpha (ECC 2.0) is experimental and may change significantly.
   LLM confidence: high

AIHot Curated Highlights
1. "我在田里雇了一名工程师，它叫 Codex" -- 北海道一个西兰花农的 8 个真实 AI 用法
   Source: aihot | Type: curated_trend
   URL: https://x.com/AYi_AInotes/status/2063573709672104286
   Signal captured
   Category: tools
   Summary: 日本北海道农民富安弘毅用 ChatGPT 和 Codex 解决农场实际问题，涵盖 8 个用法：拍照识别西兰花病害、卫星监测获取 NDVI 数据、用 ESP32 和 LINE 机器人远程控制温室卷帘、为农场群聊开发机器人管理温度与排期、从聊天记录追踪播种数量、学习 RTK-GPS 自动转向原理并评估自建成本、设计基于 Airtable 的农场管理数据库。他说 AI 让传统昂贵的自动化变得低成本可及，"如同身边有一位超级工程师"。

2. Her · हेर - Claude Code 会话分析工具
   Source: aihot | Type: curated_trend
   URL: https://huggingface.co/blog/build-small-hackathon/her-blog
   Signal captured
   Category: products
   Summary: Her（हेर）是专为 Claude Code 设计的会话分析工具。用户上传 `.jsonl` 文件后，Her 用自然语言重建每轮交互，标记部署、配置变更、秘密等高风险操作并定位到具体轮次。它展示 token 消耗、所用工具、子智能体、技能和 MCP 服务器，并结合 Anthropic 与社区最佳实践给出改进建议（仅在有明确可修复模式时）。内置"Ask Her"问答功能，支持单会话与跨会话项目分析。工具不调用第三方 AI API，使用 Nemotron-Mini-4B-Instruct 模型在 Hugging Face ZeroGPU 上运行，评估引擎完全确定，模型仅负责文本生成与建议。Her 内置 Homebrew、npm、PyPI 主流 CLI 工具数据库，自动识别会话中使用的工具，并对部署工具、数据库客户端等执行活动进行标记提醒。

3. NVIDIA 与 KRAFTON、NC、T1 在韩国 PC 房庆祝 RTX Spark 发布

10. 五个实验室，五个心智：用小模型构建多模型金融剧情游戏
   Source: aihot | Type: curated_trend
   URL: https://huggingface.co/blog/build-small-hackathon/thousand-token-wood-sim-v2
   Signal captured
   Category: tools
   Summary: Thousand Token Wood v2使用四个不同实验室的小模型（gpt-oss-20b、MiniCPM3-4B、Nemotron-Mini-4B及微调Qwen 0.5B）驱动金融模拟游戏的智能体。核心发现是异构服务层摩擦在于vLLM 0.22.1需CUDA工具包，而非模型本身。通过容忍性JSON解析层，添加模型只需一条配置。信息隔离确保内幕标志不在提示词中，扫描测试验证无泄露。记忆用情绪摘要截断避免淹没。微调0.5B模型实现0%自成交、100%有效报价，真相防火墙零泄露。小模型是可靠格式生成器但不可靠推理器，可通过结构化、提示词和微调弥补。
   Original: Hugging Face：Blog（RSS） - https://huggingface.co/blog/build-small-hackathon/thousand-token-wood-sim-v2

13. GitHub 开源 Spec Kit 工具包，用产品规范引导 AI 编码
   Source: aihot | Type: curated_trend
   URL: https://x.com/rohanpaul_ai/status/2063246343842501091
   Signal captured
   Category: products
   Summary: GitHub 发布开源工具包 Spec Kit，旨在解决 "vibe coding" 的最大弱点--AI 常在规则未明确时就开始编码。它把流程从 "让 AI 直接构建" 改为 "先写产品规范，再让 AI 根据规范实现"。当前 AI 编码模式常因松散提示直接跳入代码，导致需求薄弱、边界遗漏和反复返工。Spec Kit 推动反向流程：先定义产品功能，再澄清差距、制订技术计划、分解任务，最后让 agent 执行。规范成为可执行的开发合约，支持 Copilot、Claude Code、Codex、Gemini、Cursor、Qwen 等 30+ agent 集成。项目已获 109K+ 星标。
   Original: X：Rohan Paul (@rohanpaul_ai) - https://x.com/rohanpaul_ai/status/2063246343842501091
   Why it matters: GitHub 发布开源工具包 Spec Kit，旨在解决 "vibe coding" 的最大弱点--AI 常在规则未明确时就开始编码。它把流程从 "让 AI 直接构建" 改为 "先写产品规范，再让 AI 根据规范实现"。当前 AI 编码模式常因松散提示直接跳入代码，导致需求薄弱、边界遗漏和反复返工。Spec Kit 推动反向流程：先定义产品功能，再澄清差距、制订技术计划、分解任务，最后让 agent 执行。规范成为可执行的开发合约，支持 Copilot、Claude Code、Codex、Gemini、Cursor、Qwen 等 30+ agent 集成。项目已获 109K+ 星标。

15. Persona Atlas：Hugging Face 上的开源人物思维映射工具
   Source: aihot | Type: curated_trend
   URL: https://huggingface.co/blog/build-small-hackathon/persona-atlas
   Signal captured
   Category: products
   Summary: Persona Atlas 是一个运行在 Hugging Face Inference Providers 上的开源项目。它通过工具调用代理执行真实网络搜索，生成公众人物的资料、事实清单和风格假设，然后让该人物回答十个关于身份、伦理等开放式问题。每个回答被转化为嵌入向量，从而在向量空间中对不同人物进行距离比较，并基于十个特质锚点绘制热力图。前端采用 Gradio，提供研究、比较和检查代理完整追溯三个标签页，预设多个人物角色，无需 token 即可直接体验。
   Original: Hugging Face：Blog（RSS） - https://huggingface.co/blog/build-small-hackathon/persona-atlas
   Why it matters: Persona Atlas 是一个运行在 Hugging Face Inference Providers 上的开源项目。它通过工具调用代理执行真实网络搜索，生成公众人物的资料、事实清单和风格假设，然后让该人物回答十个关于身份、伦理等开放式问题。每个回答被转化为嵌入向量，从而在向量空间中对不同人物进行距离比较，并基于十个特质锚点绘制热力图。前端采用 Gradio，提供研究、比较和检查代理完整追溯三个标签页，预设多个人物角色，无需 token 即可直接体验。

数据说明：
- GitHub API 只提供当前 stars，总量变化来自本项目保存的历史 snapshot。
- daily delta 使用约 24h 前 snapshot，weekly delta 使用约 7 天前 snapshot。
- Accelerating 只展示至少 3 天历史基线且今日 delta 超过前 3 日均值 2 倍的项目。
- Potential Score 为规则评分，用于排序，不代表项目质量结论。

反馈摘要：
- 本周反馈 0 条：有用 0，不相关 0，已看 0。
- 本周有用方向：暂无。
